You said

optimize this video fro webistye on phone screen

Gemini said

To optimize this video for a mobile website, you should focus on balancing visual impact with performance, ensuring it loads quickly and displays correctly on smaller screens.

1. Aspect Ratio & Framing

Since your target is a phone screen, the current wide-format video (16:9) will appear very small when viewed in portrait mode.

  • Reframe to Vertical (9:16): Crop the video to a vertical orientation. This ensures the content fills the user's screen, making it much more engaging.

  • Maintain Focus: Ensure the key elements—the medical device, data visualizations, and text—remain centered after cropping.

2. File Compression & Encoding

Mobile devices require lightweight files to prevent slow load times, which can negatively impact your website's search ranking and user experience.

  • Format: Use MP4 (H.264) for maximum compatibility, or WebM for superior compression if you are targeting modern browsers.

  • Resolution: A resolution of 720p (720x1280) is usually sufficient for mobile screens and significantly reduces file size compared to 1080p or 4K.

  • Bitrate: Use a variable bitrate (VBR) to keep the file size under 5–8 MB if possible.

3. Website Implementation

How you embed the video matters for both performance and user experience.

  • Use the <video> tag: To ensure the video plays automatically on mobile, use these attributes:

    HTML
    <video autoplay muted loop playsinline poster="your-image.jpg">
  <source src="your-video.mp4" type="video/mp4">
</video>

    *   **`muted` and `autoplay`:** Required for browsers to allow automatic playback.
    *   **`playsinline`:** Essential to prevent the phone from forcing the video to full-screen mode on iOS.
    *   **`poster`:** Provide a high-quality static image to show while the video loads.
*   **Lazy Loading:** If the video is not at the very top of the page, use lazy loading to ensure it only starts downloading when the user scrolls near it.

### 4. Accessibility
*   **Text Size:** Because you are cropping to a vertical format, ensure any overlay text (like "Rapid Detection") remains legible. If it becomes too small, consider omitting the text and using your website's HTML text overlays instead.
*   **Contrast:** The current dark, high-contrast aesthetic is excellent for mobile visibility; maintain this to ensure it stands out against various background lighting conditions.

Would you like me to help you draft the HTML/CSS code snippet to embed this video on your website?
